title: awesome-low-level-design
description: Learn Low Level Design (LLD) and prepare for interviews using free resources.
notes: High-star curated collection focused on low-level design and interview prep; active and well-maintained.

title: low-level-design-primer
description: Dedicated Resources for the Low-Level System Design. Learn how to design and implement large-scale systems. Prep for the system design interview.
notes: Comprehensive primer for system/low-level design; useful for interviews and architecture learning.

title: Bolts-ObjC
description: Bolts is a collection of low-level libraries designed to make developing mobile apps easier.
notes: Mature low-level mobile helper libraries; lower recent activity but still referenced.

title: Bolts-Android
description: Bolts is a collection of low-level libraries designed to make developing mobile apps easier.
notes: Archived Android version of Bolts; still useful historically but not actively maintained.

title: DeepResearchAgent
description: A hierarchical multi-agent system for task decomposition and research automation.
notes: Active framework implementing hierarchical multi-agent coordination; relevant if collecting AI/automation tools.

title: Eureka
description: Official repository for research 'Eureka: Human-Level Reward Design via Coding Large Language Models' (ICLR 2024).
notes: Research codebase tied to a paper; useful for academic/research references.

title: Data-Structure-Algorithms-LLD-HLD
description: A Data Structure Algorithms Low Level Design and High Level Design collection of resources.
notes: Good collection for algorithm design and interview prep; maintained.

title: python-coding-interview
description: A middle-to-high level open source algorithm book designed with coding interview at heart.
notes: Open-source algorithm book in TeX; good for study and reference.

title: Claude-Red
description: Curated library of offensive security skills for the Claude skills system.
notes: Security-focused collection of 'skills'; include with caution and awareness of intent.

title: System-Design
description: Design-level considerations for scalability, maintainability, availability and reliability; interview prep.
notes: Broad system design resource; good for architecture study.

title: awesome
description: Awesome lists about all kinds of interesting topics.
notes: General curated collection of 'awesome' lists; high-level resource hub. Limited direct relevance to level design but useful for finding curated resources.

title: freeCodeCamp
description: freeCodeCamp.org's open-source codebase and curriculum. Learn math, programming, and computer science for free.
notes: Comprehensive learning platform; useful background material but not specific to level design. Good for learning programming skills useful in game dev.

title: public-apis
description: A collective list of free APIs.
notes: Large directory of public APIs. Can be used to find tools/services relevant to game design (maps, analytics, etc.) but not level-design-specific.

title: system-design-primer
description: Learn how to design large-scale systems. Prep for the system design interview. Includes Anki flashcards.
notes: Great resource for software/system design principles; indirectly useful for game backend/architecture but not level design.

title: coding-interview-university
description: A complete computer science study plan to become a software engineer.
notes: CS study plan — useful for strengthening foundational skills used in game development, not level-design-specific.

title: awesome-python
description: An opinionated list of Python frameworks, libraries, tools, and resources.
notes: Language-specific resource collection — useful when Python tools or scripts are needed in a level-design pipeline.

title: awesome-selfhosted
description: A list of Free Software network services and web applications which can be hosted on your own servers.
notes: Infrastructure-focused list — useful for hosting tools (asset servers, analytics) that could support level design workflows.

title: the-book-of-secret-knowledge
description: A collection of inspiring lists, manuals, cheatsheets, blogs, hacks, one-liners, cli/web tools and more.
notes: Large grab-bag of practical resources; may contain useful utilities for tooling around level design.

title: ECC
description: The agent harness performance optimization system. Skills, instincts, memory, security, and research-first development for Claude Code, Codex, Opencode, Cursor and beyond.
notes: Developer tooling for agent/AI workflows — could be useful for procedurally-assisted level design or automation.

title: computer-science
description: Path to a free self-taught education in Computer Science!
notes: Full CS curriculum useful for foundational knowledge; not specific to level design but helpful for broader skill-building.
